content:
Hello there Roger Clarkson.




Can you provide us with more details?

Can you provide us with more details? Please list what antivirus you use, what VPN software you are using, and whether your SMTP settings are correct.This issue may be caused by outdated stored credentials, incorrect server settings, or the need to generate an app-specific password. Correct Thunderbird Settings:> Server name: mail.btinternet.com> Port: 465 or 587 Connection security: SSL/TLS (for 465) or START-TLS (for 587) Authentication method: Normal password User name: Full email address (yourname@btinternet.com)Misspelled entries often cause Thunderbird to reject them.


Asked details : Security Check:Security software?OS: Mac or Windows?Are password combinations secure?Can they be changed ?do you use browser or desktop app?Do you use two step authentication in Btmail?


Reset Try opening Settings (the three lines icon on Windows or 'Preferences' on Mac). Go to Privacy & Security and scroll down to the Passwords section.Remove old credentials: Click on 'Saved Passwords', then 'Show Passwords'. Find all entries for mail.btinternet.com (both incoming and outgoing) and click Remove.Restart: Close and reopen Thunderbird. It should now prompt you for your password. Type it in carefully.Important: Use the same password you use to log in to BT Webmail and ensure the username is your full email address.


Cache Can you clear the browser cache? And all cookies?

content:
Filter rules are stored in a msgFilterRules.dat file for each email account inside the Thunderbird profile. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/profiles-where-thunderbird-stores-user-data

You can copy those file(s) from the old machine, and paste them to the corresponding location on the new machine. Do that while Thunderbird is closed.
creator: christ1

content:
Hello there



Locate the msgfilterRules.dat file Locate the msgFilterRules.dat file on the old computer.Windows Location: %appdata%\Thunderbird\Profiles\xxxxxxxx.default\ImapMail\imap.<account>\

Steps:Copy the msgFilterRules.dat file to a USB drive or cloud storage.Paste the file into the corresponding account folder on the new computer while Thunderbird is closed.Restart Thunderbird on the computer machine.

Alternative Method Open Thunderbird, go to Help > Troubleshooting Information.Under the "Profile Directory" row, click Open Folder (or Show in Finder/Folder).Browse into the Mail (for POP) or ImapMail (for IMAP) folder to find the file.Migration Steps:Copy the msgFilterRules.dat file to a USB drive or cloud storage.Paste the file into the corresponding account folder on the new computer while Thunderbird is closed.Restart Thunderbird on the computer machine.

content:
Hello there



Can I ask you to copy these files again while Thunderbird is not running at all? If Thunderbird is running when you make the copies, the files can become corrupted. The files were copied while Thunderbird was still running on one of these PCs.

The msgFilterRules.dat file has become corrupted or changed with valid/invalid characters/symbols. Because Thunderbird cannot read these, it ignores the file, leading to disappearing filters.

Use Please try using this add-on to resolve this issue. https://addons.thunderbird.net/en-us/thunderbird/addon/importexporttools-ng/versions/?page=1#version-14.1.18

Tip Try to delete the newer version on the new PC and reinstall it. It might be that you are using a changed or otherwise installed Thunderbird installation.

content:
If it's a well-known email provider, Thunderbird may know the proper server settings based on your email address, and do a good basic job of setting it up for you. You will be prompted through this when you first install Thunderbird. After that, go to <Account Settings> and click the "New Account" button.

If this is a less well-know provider or the automatic process fails, gather the correct settings from their support web site. You will need to know:

for incoming: Protocol (IMAP, in this case) Hostname/server name Port Connection security (STARTTLS or SSL/TLS) Authentication method (usually Normal Password or OAuth2)

Same info needed for the outgoing (SMTP) server. 

Then start the New Account process, and after you enter your email address, you'll be presented with a "Configure manually" option where you can enter the server information you gathered.
creator: rednoise

- Make sure cookies are allowed in Thunderbird

- Do not use a VPN while attempting to authenticate your Google account
